calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Receipts On Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-02-2020 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-08-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Lee, Esq., counsel for Carole Wagner and Michelle Howard, Co-Executors, (appearing by video conference).

The Court notes with some concern certain unauthorized preliminary distributions made by Co-Executor Wagner to herself. Mr. Lee confirms the same but notes that the Petition was pending hearing before the pandemic, exigent circumstances arose during the course of shelter-in-place, and Ms. Wagner is the sole beneficiary of the estate so no harm was occasioned to any other person or entity. The Court declines the "no harm, no foul" argument but will nonetheless take the matter under submission.

The Court notes that the requested attorney fees are less than the statutory fee. Mr. Lee advises the Court that counsel consents to a reduced fee in order to forego the filing of additional pleadings.

The Court takes the matter under submission with the stated intention to grant the same as modified. The Court sets hearing on the status of the filing of receipts on distribution for December 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept, 204. No appearance is required if the receipts are on file prior to that date
.
Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-15-2020 Continued To Oct-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-18-2020 Continued To Sep-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Lee appeared by telephone for Michelle Howard and Carole Wagner.

The Court stated that it needs a written response to the Probate Examiner's notes and new proposed order. Mr. Lee apologized to the Court stating that he prepared a response, but he did not get it filed and that he did not give notice of the amendment. Mr. Lee also stated that the two Petitioners will waive notice, that the amount that was owed to the City of San Francisco was paid out, that he did not get the City of San Francisco to withdraw their request for special notice and that they have a second amended petition that needs to get filed. The Court informed Counsel that if the request for fees is beyond the inventory and appraisal and based on receipts, gains or losses, they will need to file schedules. Mr. Lee stated that he does believe they are requesting extraordinary fees, that he prepared a declaration and that he believes they are just asking for statutory fees. The Court informed Counsel that if they want the additional $700.00, they will need to file schedules. The Frist and Final Report of Co-Executors on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to September 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-20-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-23-2019 Continued To Nov-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Richard James Lee for Carole A. Wagner.

Mr. Lee stated that the date for this status hearing on the close of the estate was not in the order. The Court admonished counsel stating that the status date is set at the time that the personal representative is appointed and that the time frames are also set forth in the statute. Mr. Lee requested a 30-day continuance stating that they are attempting to sell the house in the North. The Court continued the status on the filing of the Petition for Final Distribution or Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration to November 20, 2109 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: M. Nuque. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-27-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richar Lee for Carole Wagner and Michelle Howard (Petitioners)

Mr. Lee stated he does not expect any appearances from anyone objecting. Mr. Lee also questioned the amount of bond the Court may be requiring and stated that one of the Petitioners resides out of state. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will and Letters Testamentary is Taken Under Submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
